column88: The primary outcome was overall survival assessed in the intention-to-treat population.
column121: Secondary outcomes were prostate cancer-specific survival (defined as the time from randomisation to death from prostate cancer); failure-free survival (defined as biochemical failure, local or lymph node progression, distant metastases, or death from prostate cancer); progression-free survival (defined as failure-free survival but excluding biochemical failure); metastatic progression-free survival (defined as progression of or new distant metastases or death from prostate cancer); symptomatic skeletal-related events (defined as pathological fracture or spinal cord compression or bone pain requiring radiotherapy or surgery); toxic effects and adverse events; and cost-effectiveness of research treatment.
column126: Standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy) plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg orally once daily and prednisolone 5 mg orally once daily (in the abiraterone trial), administered until radiographical progression or another reason for discontinuation.
column127: Standard of care plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg (in the abiraterone trial) orally; abiraterone acetate and prednisolone plus enzalutamide 160 mg orally once a day (in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial)
column128: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (abiraterone) with or without enzalutamide, each added to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy with or without docetaxel)
column130: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone with or without enzalutamide for patients with metastatic prostate cancer starting androgen deprivation therapy (specifically, treatment arms: standard of care [androgen deprivation therapy; docetaxel 75 mg/m² intravenously for six cycles with prednisolone 10 mg orally once per day allowed] or standard of care plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg orally once per day, or abiraterone acetate and prednisolone plus enzalutamide 160 mg orally once a day)
